title: REPLACE ME — candidate classic rock fact
body: Use this record shape for a curated fact tied to a day or week. Keep it to 2 or 3 sentences, and make sure the wording is friendly enough for Daily Flyer.
notes: Replace this template after you approve real facts.

title: REPLACE ME — candidate Irish history fact
body: This is a placeholder structure for an Irish history card. When you add the real fact, keep the body concise and educational, with source-backed dates.
notes: Good category for day-of and week-of facts.

title: REPLACE ME — candidate Boston sports fact
body: Use Boston sports facts for Red Sox, Bruins, Celtics, or Patriots moments that are easy to validate and still fun to read.
notes: Reference sites should be your backbone here.

title: REPLACE ME — candidate famous birthday fact
body: Famous person birthday cards should teach one interesting thing about the person, not just list a birth date.
notes: Use On This Day for discovery, then verify elsewhere.

title: REPLACE ME — candidate fun fact
body: Fun facts can be historical, cultural, or novelty day items, but the strongest ones still teach something real.
source_name: Library of Congress Today in History
notes: Use novelty-day sites as seasoning, not the backbone.

title: Mom Daily Assembly Template
body: Mom daily should sound warm, lively, and slightly rambly in a good way. Build it from already-vetted facts, family birthdays, one music tie-in, and one playful observance if it fits.
notes: This entry is a voice/style placeholder, not a final fact card.
